tweets.text: GPT-5.5 in low-reasoning mode is great — efficient and succinct. Outperforms Opus and Kimi on real-world dev tasks without needing extra compute.
GPT-5.5 low-reasoning mode is now my default for fast iteration loops.
GPT-5.5-Cyber: a security-tuned variant in limited preview for critical-infrastructure defenders. Works with Trusted Access for Cyber to detect and patch code vulnerabilities.
Announcing GPT-5.5-Cyber preview for vetted defenders of critical infrastructure.

Three concrete agentic-coding wins: 1) ad-hoc data visualisations 2) ad-hoc data annotation UIs 3) ad-hoc CLIs for existing code, with visual elements. Agents make custom dataset creation and quantitative analysis routine.
Generational split on AI input: voice for young and old, typing for middle-aged. Realtime voice progress will likely shift the middle.
people are really starting to use voice to interact with AI, especially when they have a lot of context to dump. GPT-Realtime-2 comes to the API today; it is a pretty big step forward.
Voice-to-voice translate model is wild. Multilingual realtime, full duplex.

GPT-Realtime-2 brings GPT-5-class reasoning to voice agents — real-time listening with complex problem-solving in conversation. Pairs with Realtime-Translate and Realtime-Whisper for next-gen audio interfaces.
New short course with CopilotKit on building agents that generate dynamic UIs in chat — charts, forms, whiteboards. Component-based, compositional, third-party integration patterns covered.
Re-reading classic RL papers and noting how much of recent agentic work is rediscovery with bigger compute.
